name: Rice Flan Tart with Candied Ginger
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Low-fat ricotta cheese or Pureed fat-free cottage cheese
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------LOW-FAT SWEET PASTRY----------------------------
1/2 cup Plus 2 tablespoons oat flour
5 tablespoons All-purpose flour
1/4 cup Cake flour
1/2 teaspoon Ground cardamom or cinnamon
2 1/2 tablespoons Sugar
3 teaspoons Cold butter, diced
1 tablespoon Canola oil
2 to 2 1/2 tablespoons Very cold water
The low-calorie rice pudding flan is so delicious by itself that you may want to save the calories and fat in the crust, and serve it without the pastry. Bring nonfat milk to a boil in a medium saucepan. Add vanilla bean and rice. Cover partially and simmer until rice is almost tender, about 20 to 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Stir in sugar and continue cooking for another 3 minutes. Remove from heat and cool slightly. Remove vanilla bean. Blend egg substitute with ricotta cheese. Stir into rice mixture with candied ginger. Carefully pour into prepared pastry shell, filling almost to the top (if there is any flan mixture left from incomplete reduction in cooking, place it in a small custard dish, cover and microwave for 40 to 60 seconds or until set.) Sprinkle with 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon. Bake at 400 degrees F. for 25 to 30 minutes or until set. Remove from oven. Sift powdered sugar over and sprinkle with remaining cinnamon. Serve warm and garnish with sliced ginger if desired. Makes 12 servings. Each serving contains about: 183 calories; 102 mg sodium; 11 mg cholesterol; 4 grams fat; 31 grams carbohydrates; 6 grams protein; 0.25 gram fiber. The use of oat flour blend and cake flour is what gives this low-fat crust its tenderness. It also makes it more difficult to roll, but don't despair: The dough is easily patched. Low-Fat Sweet Pastry Sift flour, cardamom and sugar into a large bowl. Rub in butter with fingers or cut in with a pastry blender until the consistency of small peas. Using a fork, stir in oil. Slowly add cold water, one teaspoon at a time, tossing with a fork until the flour is moistened without being wet. Gather dough with moist hands, shape into a flattened round and let rest about 15 minutes. Roll to a thickness of 1/8 inch on a lightly floured board. (To facilitate rolling, the dough may also be rolled between sheets of wax paper or unrolled dough may be placed in a pan and gently pressed to cover inside of the pan.) Gently lift and press into a 9-inch square tart pan with removable bottom. Cut off excess dough edges. Bake at 425 degrees F. for 10 to 15 minutes or until golden brown. Makes 12 servings. NOTE: Unsalted butter will give a more delicate fresh flavor in this pastry. Add a dash of salt if using.
